what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view E1 in area E, binding it to journey J2, platform targets, the MOD-registry, two UI addresses, and build-time outputs.
observations: This is a generated surface-spec JSON for view E1 under area E.
It sits in a journey-spine directory path (J2/E1.json).
Its home journey is J2 and it lists only J2 in journeys with no cross_journeys.
It targets desktop and mobile platforms.
It maps to UI addresses ui://inspector/model-field and ui://models.
It declares build_to components inspector and models, a design_lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline.
It has status quality-passed and an empty symbols list.
standouts: Explicitly generated by a Claude Code session with a rule to never invent an address.
Build instructions include a specific lint gate and a single-look-source token pipeline.
Represents MOD-registry and maps directly to two UI addresses.
concerns: The _what field embeds procedural provenance and rules that may drift from the file content.
cross_journeys is empty without explanation whether this surface is intentionally isolated or simply has no cross-links.
The design_lint command references canvas/app/src but the relative root is unclear from this file alone.
notes: A quality-passed surface spec that pins view E1 to journey J2, the MOD-registry, two UI addresses, and explicit build-time lint and token-source instructions.
questions: What does MOD-registry contain and how does it relate to the inspector/model-field and models addresses?
Is the empty cross_journeys intentional isolation or incomplete wiring?
Where is the root for the design_lint target path canvas/app/src relative to this file?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec for view E1 carrying address-refs for the web; the file matches that purpose by declaring the view, journey binding, addresses, and build targets.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for surface definitions
intention_signals: It tries to make a view's runtime surface, design system, and build-time validation discoverable from one declarative ledger entry.
novelty: The explicit 'never invent an address' provenance note and the tight coupling of a surface spec to a specific lint command and token-to-CSS pipeline.
contribution: A structured, build-aware surface definition that links a view to its journey, UI addresses, represented registry, platforms, and build-time quality and token sources.
summary_for_embedding: A generated JSON surface specification for view E1 in area E, bound to journey J2 and the MOD-registry, targeting desktop and mobile, referencing ui://inspector/model-field and ui://models, and declaring build outputs, a design lint gate, and a token-to-CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: To record a quality-passed view surface and its build-time design-system dependencies for the live model fleet chat and embedding area.
